action.reasoning: Observation: CLIP ViT-B/16 got 0.7380 val_acc vs ConvNeXt-tiny's 0.7560. Best model is iter_5 (0.7598 balanced_accuracy). Both models overfit severely (train_acc ~99%, val_acc ~75%). Analysis: Need stronger regularization. The ConvNeXt-tiny is better. Key: increase drop_path_rate, label_smoothing, weight_decay. Also try two-stage training: stage1 balanced sampling, stage2 logit adjustment fine-tuning. Decision: Train ConvNeXt-tiny with stronger regularization (drop_path_rate=0.3, label_smoothing=0.2, weight_decay=0.1) for 100 epochs. Also try a two-stage approach.
